about: Established in 1959 with technical and financial assistance from the former government of West Germany, IIT Madras is one of India's Institutes of National Importance. It is globally recognized for its excellence in engineering, science, humanities, and management education, consistently ranking among the top institutions in the country for its academic rigor and research output.

ug_programs.name: B.Tech in Computer Science and Engineering
B.Tech in Metallurgical and Materials Engineering
B.Tech in Naval Architecture and Ocean Engineering

pg_programs.name: M.Tech in Computer Science and Engineering
M.Tech in Data Science and AI

phd_programs.name: PhD in Computer Science and Engineering

placements.graduate_outcomes_note: IIT Madras consistently achieves high placement rates across its diverse programs. The 2022-23 placement season saw a highest international package of 1.98 Cr LPA and a highest domestic package of 1.31 Cr LPA. The average package stood at 21.48 LPA, reflecting strong demand from various sectors including IT, Consulting, Finance, and Core Engineering. These figures represent the outcomes from Phase 1 of the placement drive.

placement_highlights: IIT Madras maintains an exceptional track record in graduate employment, attracting a diverse range of top-tier companies globally. The placement season for 2022-23 (Phase 1) saw a remarkable highest international package of 1.98 Cr LPA and a highest domestic package of 1.31 Cr LPA, underscoring the high value placed on IIT Madras graduates. The average package for the same period was 21.48 LPA, with a median package of 19 LPA, indicating strong overall compensation. Over 1600 students were placed by more than 360 companies, reflecting robust industry demand. Key sectors include Information Technology, Consulting, Core Engineering, Analytics, and Finance, with prominent recruiters such as Microsoft, Google, Amazon, Intel, Goldman Sachs, McKinsey & Company, and various leading Indian conglomerates. The institute consistently strives to provide diverse opportunities, including roles in R&D, product development, data science, and management consulting, ensuring graduates are well-prepared for global careers or entrepreneurial ventures.
fees_note: The fees provided are approximate annual figures for domestic students (General/OBC/EWS categories) and include tuition, institute, and other miscellaneous charges. Tuition fees for SC/ST/PwD students are fully waived. Students whose family income is below INR 1 lakh per annum also receive a full tuition fee waiver. Students with family income between INR 1 lakh and INR 5 lakh per annum receive a 2/3rd tuition fee waiver. International students typically pay higher tuition fees, often in USD, which are significantly higher than domestic fees. The hostel fee mentioned is for room rent and establishment charges, excluding the actual mess bill which is paid separately based on consumption.

scholarships.name: Merit-cum-Means (MCM) Scholarship
scholarships.amount: Full tuition fee waiver and a monthly stipend of INR 1000
scholarships.eligibility: UG students with parental income below INR 4.5 lakhs per annum and good academic standing (CGPA of 6.0 or above)

scholarships.name: Institute Scholarship for M.Tech/MS Students
scholarships.eligibility: M.Tech/MS students admitted through GATE, fulfilling institute norms
scholarships.provider: IIT Madras (Ministry of Education, Govt. of India)

scholarships.name: Institute Scholarship for PhD Students
scholarships.amount: Monthly stipend of INR 31,000 (first two years) and INR 35,000 (subsequent years)
scholarships.eligibility: PhD students admitted through GATE/NET or with relevant degrees, fulfilling institute norms
scholarships.provider: IIT Madras (Ministry of Education, Govt. of India)

scholarships.name: Donor-funded Scholarships
scholarships.amount: Varies (can include tuition waiver, monthly stipend, or one-time grants)
scholarships.eligibility: Specific criteria set by donors, often based on merit, financial need, or program of study
scholarships.provider: Various alumni and corporate donors through IIT Madras

infrastructure.details: Dedicated blocks for various engineering and science departments, housing lecture halls, faculty offices, and administrative units.
State-of-the-art research facilities across all departments, including advanced computing labs, materials science labs, robotics labs, and bio-engineering labs.
A multi-story building with extensive collections of books, journals, and digital resources, providing a conducive environment for study and research.
Centralized computing facility providing high-performance computing resources, internet access, and software support to students and faculty.
Comprehensive sports complex including a stadium, indoor sports complex (badminton, table tennis, squash), swimming pool, gymnasium, tennis courts, and various outdoor fields.
On-campus medical facility with doctors, nurses, and ambulance services, providing primary healthcare to students and staff.
19 hostels (15 for boys, 4 for girls) providing accommodation for almost all students, with mess facilities, common rooms, and recreational areas.
On-campus shopping complex, banks, post office, food courts, and other essential services.
Multiple guest houses for visitors, parents, and conference attendees.
A sprawling, lush green campus spread over 620 acres, home to diverse flora and fauna, including deer and various bird species, providing a serene academic environment.

hostel_details.type: Primarily single rooms for senior students and double/triple rooms for freshers, with common mess facilities, recreation rooms, and internet access.

transport_details.routes: Internal bus service connects all hostels, academic zones, the main gate, and various points within the campus, operating on a regular schedule.

library_details.journals: Thousands of print journals and access to tens of thousands of e-journals
library_details.e_resources: Extensive collection of e-books, databases (e.g., IEEE, ACM, ScienceDirect, Scopus), and research tools

notable_faculty.name: V. Kamakoti
notable_faculty.specialization: Computer Science and Engineering, Computer Architecture, VLSI Design

faculty_achievements: Faculty at IIT Madras have received numerous accolades. Prof. T. Pradeep was awarded the Padma Shri for his contributions to science and engineering, particularly in nanotechnology for affordable clean water. Prof. Ashok Jhunjhunwala is recognized for his pioneering work in rural technology and telecommunications, leading initiatives like TeNeT. Former Directors like Prof. Bhaskar Ramamurthi and current Director Prof. V. Kamakoti are distinguished leaders in their respective fields of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science, guiding the institute's growth and research excellence.
recognition: Declared an Institute of National Importance by the Parliament of India.

contact_info.address: Indian Institute of Technology Madras, Chennai - 600036, Tamil Nadu, India

additional_details: Established in 1959 with technical and financial assistance from the Government of West Germany.
The campus is a protected forest, home to diverse wildlife including deer and various bird species.
Consistently ranked among the top engineering institutions in India by various ranking frameworks, including NIRF.
Pioneered the concept of a Research Park in India with the IIT Madras Research Park.
Developed India's first indigenously designed microprocessor, 'Shakti'.
Developed India's first indigenously designed standing wheelchair, 'Arise'.
